repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1: cumulant-envelope support gap chi_w(P), without the three diagnostic objects

published_prior_art.cite: Read2022 gives sign/zero algorithms; Guay2021 gives higher-moment rank checks, but not the combined diagnostic tuple

repo_prior_art.cite: parent excluded by PN1; no other catalogue LP/sign versus non-Gaussian SVAR mechanism decomposition found

published_prior_art.cite: PlagborgMollerWolf2021 + Wolf2022 + LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 + DrautzburgWright2023: closest ingredients, no multiplicative mechanism lower bound

repo_prior_art.cite: parent excluded by PN1; no other catalogue iff frontier for the three channels found

published_prior_art.cite: PlagborgMollerWolf2021 gives common-restriction equivalence; Wolf2022 and GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 give endpoints, not this iff channel frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1: parent chi_w(P) margin; theorem is a bookkeeping collapse to that parent

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The advertised closed diagnostic tau_w^mech(P) depends on c_w(P), but Assumption curvature only asserts existence of some positive constant and does not define a unique or computable functional.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: E_w^sign(P)>0 is automatic under the current normalization/Assumption sign-cone, so the stated three-channel iff does not actually encode sign-channel positivity.

soundness_flags.label: Sanity-check corollaries
soundness_flags.one_line: The PMW reduction sets R_HLP^common=R_CLP but then claims zero gap against h_SVAR when the fourth-cumulant label is imposed equally; unless the LP side is also restricted to R_SVAR, h_CLP-h_SVAR and overline Lambda need not vanish.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define c_w(P) as a canonical extremal value, such as the infimum of the support-loss-to-distance-product ratio over a specified compact local comparison set.
Rewrite Conjecture 2 so the iff uses the setwise zero-channel quantities directly, and separate sign exposure from the E=1 normalization case.
Patch the PMW corollary by defining a common-restriction LP set with the same cumulant restriction when the non-Gaussian label is imposed on both sides.

recommended_next_step: Revise the diagnostic definition and PMW sanity reduction; the novelty kernel survives but the current statements are not yet well-posed enough to accept.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft does decompose the parent chi_w(P) margin into sign-exposure, common-impact pasting, and cumulant-label channels, but the scalar diagnostic needs a precise c_w(P) definition before acceptance.
